P2 (black pot) now contains a healthy seedling (New Introduction/Intervention) and an electronic circuit board (New Introduction/Intervention). The soil in P2 appears moist. vision_report.narrative_description: The initial sequence of images (EARLIEST, T-4, T-3) depicted two pots: a light brown/yellow pot (P1) and a black pot (P2). Both contained bare, dry soil, with P2 also showing scattered white fragments, likely eggshells. No plant life was observed in these early stages. Images T-2 and T-1 suffered from a complete loss of visual data, appearing entirely black. The most recent image (CURRENT) reveals a dramatically altered scene. The light brown/yellow pot (P1) has vanished, representing a systemic loss. The black pot (P2) now hosts a single, healthy-looking seedling with three vibrant green, rounded leaves, indicating a new introduction. An electronic circuit board has also been introduced into the pot alongside the plant. The soil in P2 appears adequately moist, a change from its previously dry state. New background elements, including a blue book and a white pen, are also visible, replacing the earlier white cylindrical cup. The expected white rabbit scale anchor was not present in any of the provided images. The identity of the new plant in P2 as 'Mexican Mint' from the registry cannot be visually confirmed based on its current morphology.
